thought.text:
If-combine already simplified all conditional branches (because i=200 turned into constant after const-propagation, and `200 > k` evaluated to 1). 

Now we have lots of "br label %if.thenN ; if.thenN: ..." chains that could be merged into a single basic block (block merging / SimplifyCFG).

Let me add: **basic block merging — when a block has a single successor and that successor has a single predecessor, merge them**. This is a common SimplifyCFG transformation. It significantly reduces basic block count and exposes more optimization opportunities.

Let me also tackle that and look at current improvements. Let me also implement a "store-to-load forwarding" through PHIs and across BBs in some simple cases — but that's complex.

Let me add BB merging:

tool_call.name: file_editor

summary: Replace dead-store helper with BB merging helper
